food_name: Chicken Wings in Fish Sauce Glaze (Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm)
aliases: Fish sauce glazed fried chicken wings

english: Fish sauce glazed fried chicken wings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class; no specific entry for Vietnamese fish-sauce-glazed preparation. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for deep-fried glazed chicken wings; no specific code for Vietnamese fish sauce preparation exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm. A00LC (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm consists of approximately 4–6 chicken wing pieces, weighing around 250 g, served as an appetizer or side dish.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, cooked, fried (SR Legacy ID 05062) combined with component estimation for fish sauce glaze
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Vietnamese fish-sauce-glazed fried chicken wings exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore Foodo, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing data (FDC ID 171477 / SR Legacy 05062) with upward adjustment to sodium reflecting fish sauce glaze contribution. Input record values (energy 260 kcal, protein 18 g, fat 18 g, carbohydrate 6 g, sodium 720 mg per 100 g) are consistent with USDA fried chicken wing baseline plus fish sauce glaze sodium load and are retained as the best available estimate. Calcium 12 mg, iron 0.8 mg, cholesterol 80 mg, saturated fat 4.8 g, trans fat 0.2 g, vitamin C 2.0 mg, dietary fibre 0.4 g, and sugar 4.0 g are also consistent with USDA fried chicken wing data adjusted for glaze.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous deep-fried chicken with a small sugar-containing glaze. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with only 6 g carbohydrate per 100 g; the sugar glaze (4 g sugar/100 g) contributes a modest glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (45 × 15 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 6.75, rounded to 6.8. Available carbohydrate per serving = 6.0 g/100 g × 250 g = 15 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 720 mg per 100 g — driven by fish sauce glaze; a 250 g serving delivers approximately 1,800 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single dish.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request a significantly reduced fish sauce quantity or substitute with a low-sodium alternative, and limit the portion to no more than 100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High fat content (18 g/100 g) from deep-frying worsens insulin resistance; sugar glaze (4 g sugar/100 g) adds refined carbohydrate; glycemic load per serving is moderate at 6.8.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g, request baked or air-fried preparation with reduced glaze to lower fat and sugar intake, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate the overall glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high total fat (18 g/100 g), saturated fat (4.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), cholesterol (80 mg/100 g), and very high sodium (720 mg/100 g) creates a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this dish; the saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium levels collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk, and a 250 g serving provides 12 g saturated fat — 60% of the recommended daily limit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; sodium at 720 mg/100 g exceeds the RA-sensitive threshold and worsens tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; deep-fried preparations are strongly pro-inflammatory, and those with nightshade sensitivity should additionally avoid the chilli component.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm (Chicken Wings in Fish Sauce Glaze) is a popular Vietnamese appetizer and side dish consisting of chicken wings that are marinated, deep-fried until golden and crispy, then tossed in a caramelised glaze made from fish sauce, sugar, garlic, and chilli. The result is a dish with a deeply savoury, slightly sweet, and umami-rich flavour profile with a sticky, lacquered exterior and juicy interior. It is widely served in Vietnamese restaurants and hawker-style eateries across Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 260 kcal, 18 g protein, 18 g fat (4.8 g saturated, 0.2 g trans), and 6 g carbohydrate (4 g sugar), with a very high sodium content of 720 mg driven by the fish sauce glaze. The dish is clinically significant for hypertension management due to its sodium load, and for cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol content. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category) owing to the dominant protein-fat matrix, though the sugar glaze contributes a moderate glycemic load of approximately 6.8 per 250 g serving. It is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis, and should be consumed with caution by those with Type 2 Diabetes. Fish sauce is the primary allergen driver (fish). The dish is dairy-free but not vegetarian, vegan, or reliably halal without verified fish sauce sourcing.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Cánh Gà Chiên Nước Mắm exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing data with sodium adjusted upward to reflect fish sauce glaze contribution. The input record values are internally consistent and plausible for this dish type. Confidence is medium because the values are estimated from a close analogue rather than directly measured for this specific preparation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese fish-sauce-glazed fried chicken wing preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, HPB Singapore Foodo, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases as of April 2026. No zero-macro justification required as protein_g (18.0), fat_g (18.0), and carbohydrate_g (6.0) are all non-zero. Halal status is unverified as commercial fish sauce may contain non-halal processing aids; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant or product. The input record listed 'country_of_origin' as Singapore, which reflects the serving context (GrabFood Singapore); the dish's culinary origin is Vietnam and is recorded accordingly. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026.
